problem: Weak core muscles unable to support the spine, tight hip flexors pulling on the lower back, poor posture during sitting and exercise, overloading the spine before core is strong enough
solution: Strengthen core gradually, stretch hip flexors daily, avoid heavy spine-loading, replace heavy exercises with lighter alternatives, always warm up the back
precautions: Stop immediately if pain radiates down the leg (nerve involvement)
Sleep with a pillow between knees if side sleeper
Avoid prolonged sitting — stand every 45 minutes

problem: Sedentary lifestyle, insufficient protein intake, poor sleep, low muscle mass
solution: Progressive overload, compound movements, protein intake, sleep, eat in a caloric surplus
Start with 15-20 minute walks, progress to brisk walking, then jogging intervals, focus on strength first
Eat in a surplus, prioritize protein, add calorie-dense foods, eat 5-6 smaller meals, liquid calories help
exercises: Limit cardio to 1-2x per week (don't burn the surplus)
Rest 48-72 hours between training the same muscle group
diet_tips: Milk + banana + peanut butter smoothie (~500 kcal, 20g protein)
Paneer bhurji with 3 rotis (~600 kcal, 30g protein)
Chicken curry + 2 cups rice (~700 kcal, 40g protein)
Dal makhani + rice + curd (~550 kcal, 22g protein)
Egg bhurji (4 eggs) + toast (~400 kcal, 28g protein)

solution: Daily chest stretches, band pull-aparts daily, strengthen upper back, avoid excessive phone/screen hunching, add wall angels to morning routine

Protein within 30-60 minutes, hydrate well, light stretching or walk the next day (active recovery), pain level 0-3: continue with modifications, pain level 4-6: reduce intensity by 50%, focus on mobility, pain level 7+: rest, treat the issue, consult a professional if persists
